description_long: This is a brass trumpet with a classic, elongated cylindrical body and a wide, flared bell at one end. The instrument features three piston valves arranged in a row along the top, each with a round button for finger actuation. The mouthpiece is attached at the narrow end, leading into a gently curved leadpipe that connects to the main tuning slide. The tubing is coiled in a compact, double-looped configuration, typical of standard trumpets, and the surface has a glossy, metallic finish that reflects light. The bell is smoothly flared and dominates one end of the instrument, while the opposite end is more narrow and fitted with the mouthpiece. The overall appearance is sleek and polished, with a dark, reflective brass color.
description: A polished brass trumpet with a wide flared bell, three piston valves, and a gently curved leadpipe. The surface is glossy and reflective, showcasing a classic, compact trumpet design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the trumpet, the three piston valves, and the wide flared bell.
description_view_1: Front view looking directly into the bell, highlighting its circular opening and the alignment of the tubing behind it.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the trumpet's length, valve arrangement, and the mouthpiece at the narrow end.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the mouthpiece and the coiled tubing, with the bell visible at the far end.
attribute_bell_flare: The bell is wide and smoothly flared, creating a gradual, elegant opening at the end.
attribute_valve_configuration: Three piston valves are arranged in a straight line along the top center of the trumpet.
attribute_leadpipe_curvature: The leadpipe is gently curved, connecting the mouthpiece to the main tuning slide in a smooth arc.
attribute_tuning_slide_location: The main tuning slide is U-shaped and located just after the leadpipe, before the valve section, oriented horizontally.
